about:
Hey there! My name is Sunny and I’m a finance and stats student at UofT. 
This ML blog is just a fun little hobby of mine where I informally document my findings and share my progress in machine learning.
I don’t have much of a background in statistics nor data science, but I’m very eager to learn more. 
It's chill, honest, and hopefully helpful to anyone on a similar path!
Special shout out to my brother (CS major) and sister (Data Scientist) who have helped me with my work and given me advice.

objectives.text: Learn the fundamental concepts of ML.
Complete a full end-to-end ML project: data collection, preprocessing, training, evaluation, and deployment.
What in the world is a hyperparameter and how to decide which one to use?
If everyone uses the same model for a given problem, then how can they improve their accuracy above the others? Is it mostly feature engineering?
How do people think of unique features to create? Are there resources to learn more about this?
What is a tree model and how does increasing or decreasing the depth affect the model?
What are the ideal accuracy rates for a model to achieve realistically? Does this vary in different industries or is there a standard rate?

items.name: Andrew Ng's Machine Learning Specialization
items.description: The classic ML course, updated for 2024. Great starting point.

items.name: fast.ai Practical Deep Learning
items.description: Top-down approach to deep learning. Very hands-on.

items.name: Hands-On Machine Learning (Aurelien Geron)
items.description: Practical guide with scikit-learn, Keras, and TensorFlow.

items.name: Deep Learning (Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville)
items.description: The deep learning bible. Dense but comprehensive.

items.name: PyTorch
items.description: My go-to framework for building and training models.

items.name: Hugging Face
items.description: Pretrained models, datasets, and a great community.
